title: Bootstrap
rules: move feature logic out of bootstrap

primary_when: the request is specifically about composition root, startup sequence, or entrypoint setup
the request is about deterministic startup order
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about UI layout, runtime patching, or assembly parsing

solves: how to bring runtime systems up in a deterministic order
how to keep composition root logic explicit and reviewable
how to separate startup orchestration from feature implementation

core_principles: Bootstrap should orchestrate startup, not absorb feature behavior.
Initialization order must be explicit and stable.
Diagnostics should be available before optional systems start.
constraints: Startup dependencies must be reviewable without executing hidden registration logic.
Optional systems must not become implicit prerequisites accidentally.
Composition root logic should remain thin enough to inspect quickly.
decision_rules: Use bootstrap as the owner when startup order, entrypoint wiring, or composition root policy dominates the task.
Push boundary contracts into contracts before binding implementations in bootstrap.
Keep one authoritative startup path per host or runtime surface.

avoid_patterns: startup side effects hidden in registration callbacks
multiple competing bootstrap paths for one runtime host

recipes.name: Build a deterministic composition root
recipes.when: Use when a runtime host must start core systems in a reviewable order.
steps: Define the entrypoint boundary and startup phases.
Start systems in one explicit sequence.

pitfalls: Growing bootstrap until it owns feature logic.
Initializing optional services before core diagnostics.
Depending on registration side effects to determine startup order.
review_checklist: Is composition root thin enough to review quickly?
Have feature concerns been kept out of bootstrap?

references.title: Local Reference: Framework Bootstrap
references.interpretation: Use this first for startup-phase design, composition root boundaries, and deterministic initialization policy.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for explicit bootstrap and startup ordering.

references.title: Local Reference: Framework Diagnostics
references.interpretation: Use this to align early bootstrap with diagnostics initialization and failure visibility.
references.why: Use to ensure bootstrap initializes diagnostics at the correct point in startup.
